# Test fixture manifest

Total size: 19 MB (`du -sh tests/fixtures`). No NC/ND-licensed or LDC-sourced
material is present.

## Audio — `tests/fixtures/audio/`

### `arctic_bdl_a0001.wav`

- Source: CMU_ARCTIC, `cmu_us_bdl_arctic`, utterance `arctic_a0001`
  ("Author of the danger trail, Philip Steels, etc."), male US English
  speaker.
- URL: <http://festvox.org/cmu_arctic/cmu_arctic/cmu_us_bdl_arctic/wav/arctic_a0001.wav>
- License: CMU_ARCTIC license (Carnegie Mellon University, Copyright (c)
  2003, All Rights Reserved) — "free for use for any purpose (commercial or
  otherwise)"; permission to use, copy, modify, and license without fee,
  conditional on retaining the copyright notice, marking modifications, and
  keeping the original authors' names. Full text at
  <http://festvox.org/cmu_arctic/cmu_arctic/cmu_us_bdl_arctic/COPYING>.
- Processing: none. 16 kHz, 16-bit PCM, mono, as distributed.

### `arctic_slt_a0001.wav`

- Source: CMU_ARCTIC, `cmu_us_slt_arctic`, utterance `arctic_a0001` ("Not
  at this particular case, Tom, apologized Whittemore."), female US
  English speaker.
- URL: <http://festvox.org/cmu_arctic/cmu_arctic/cmu_us_slt_arctic/wav/arctic_a0001.wav>
- License: CMU_ARCTIC license, identical text to the `bdl` COPYING file
  above. Full text at
  <http://festvox.org/cmu_arctic/cmu_arctic/cmu_us_slt_arctic/COPYING>.
- Processing: none. 16 kHz, 16-bit PCM, mono, as distributed.

### `librispeech_2277-149896-0005.wav`

- Source: LibriSpeech corpus, `dev-clean` subset, speaker 2277 (female,
  per `SPEAKERS.TXT`), chapter 149896, utterance
  `2277-149896-0005` ("many little wrinkles gathered between his eyes as
  he contemplated this and his brow moistened"). LibriSpeech audio derives
  from public-domain LibriVox audiobook recordings.
- URL: <https://www.openslr.org/resources/12/dev-clean.tar.gz> (OpenSLR
  resource 12), archive member
  `LibriSpeech/dev-clean/2277/149896/2277-149896-0005.flac`.
- License: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0), per
  `LibriSpeech/LICENSE.TXT` in the same archive ("LibriSpeech (c) 2014 by
  Vassil Panayotov ... licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
  International License"). License text: <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/>.
- Attribution: LibriSpeech corpus by Vassil Panayotov et al.
- Processing: decoded from FLAC to 16-bit PCM WAV with `ffmpeg`
  (`-c:a pcm_s16le`); no resampling (source already 16 kHz mono), no
  trimming beyond the corpus's existing utterance boundary.

### `synth_vowel_a.wav`

- Source: generated by `tests/fixtures/scripts/synth_vowel.py`, an
  original source-filter formant synthesizer written for this repository —
  a glottal impulse train at 110 Hz through a cascade of four second-order
  resonators tuned to the average adult-male /a/ formants reported in
  Peterson & Barney (1952): F1 730 Hz, F2 1090 Hz, F3 2440 Hz, F4 3400 Hz;
  bandwidths per the Fant (1960) convention BW = 50 + F/10 Hz.
  Deterministic given seed `20260716`.
- License: original work, project license (MIT OR Apache-2.0).
- Processing: n/a (synthesized). 16 kHz, 16-bit PCM, mono, 2.0 s.

### `synth_tone_sweep.wav`

- Source: generated by `tests/fixtures/scripts/synth_sweep.py`, a
  logarithmic chirp from 50 Hz to 7500 Hz over 5 s
  (`scipy.signal.chirp(method="logarithmic")`). No randomness in the
  signal path.
- License: original work, project license (MIT OR Apache-2.0).
- Processing: n/a (synthesized). 16 kHz, 16-bit PCM, mono, 5.0 s.

### `long_scroll_test.wav`

- Source: generated by `tests/fixtures/scripts/build_long_fixture.py`,
  concatenating `arctic_bdl_a0001.wav`, `arctic_slt_a0001.wav`,
  `librispeech_2277-149896-0005.wav`, `synth_vowel_a.wav`, and
  `synth_tone_sweep.wav`, separated by 0.5 s of silence with a 1.0 s gap
  between repeats of the sequence, looped to reach 600 s, trimmed to
  exactly 600 s, with a 0.5 s linear fade-out at the end.
- License: aggregate of the licenses of its five component clips (see
  their entries above); the CC BY 4.0 portion carries through and requires
  the same LibriSpeech attribution.
- Processing: concatenation, looping, trimming, fade-out, as described
  above. 16 kHz, 16-bit PCM, mono, 600.0 s (19.2 MB).

## TextGrids — `tests/fixtures/textgrids/`

Redistributable real-world TextGrid corpora with a clear license are not
available. All three files below are hand-authored for this repository,
following the file-format description in the Praat manual page "TextGrid
file formats"
(<https://www.fon.hum.uva.nl/praat/manual/TextGrid_file_formats.html>).
Word and phone interval boundaries are placed by hand for format-testing
purposes; they are not the output of acoustic alignment and should not be
treated as a phonetic ground truth.

### `arctic_bdl_a0001_long_utf8.TextGrid`

- Authored fixture, annotating `arctic_bdl_a0001.wav`.
- License: original work, project license (MIT OR Apache-2.0).
- Format: TextGrid long (full) text format, UTF-8 without byte-order mark.
- Content: `words` interval tier (9 intervals, including one empty
  interval for a pause), `phones` interval tier with IPA labels, `events`
  point tier (2 points).

### `arctic_slt_a0001_short_utf8.TextGrid`

- Authored fixture, annotating `arctic_slt_a0001.wav`.
- License: original work, project license (MIT OR Apache-2.0).
- Format: TextGrid short text format, UTF-8 without byte-order mark.
- Content: single `words` interval tier (9 intervals, including one empty
  interval for a pause).

### `librispeech_2277-149896-0005_long_utf16.TextGrid`

- Authored fixture, annotating `librispeech_2277-149896-0005.wav`.
- License: original work, project license (MIT OR Apache-2.0).
- Format: TextGrid long (full) text format, UTF-16LE with byte-order mark
  (`FF FE`), per the manual's encoding section (§7): "you can use ...
  UTF-16 encoding (either Big-Endian or Little-Endian, with Byte Order
  Mark)".
- Content: `words` interval tier and `phones` interval tier with IPA
  labels (16 intervals each, including one empty interval for a
  mid-utterance pause).

## Scripts — `tests/fixtures/scripts/`

- `synth_vowel.py` — generates `synth_vowel_a.wav`. `uv run` script
  (PEP 723 header), dependencies `numpy`, `soundfile`. Seeded (`20260716`).
- `synth_sweep.py` — generates `synth_tone_sweep.wav`. `uv run` script,
  dependencies `numpy`, `scipy`, `soundfile`. No randomness.
- `build_long_fixture.py` — generates `long_scroll_test.wav` from the
  other five audio clips. `uv run` script, dependencies `numpy`,
  `soundfile`. Run order: `synth_vowel.py`, `synth_sweep.py`, then this
  script.

All three are executable (`uv run tests/fixtures/scripts/<name>.py`) and
regenerate their output deterministically.
